recalls.Description: Persilux is recalling its zebra blinds due to serious safety hazards related to long operating cords that pose a strangulation and entanglement risk to children. These blinds, available in various colors including beige, black, dark gray, light gray, and white, range in size from 22 to 73 inches wide and 64 to 72 inches high. The product packaging features the names 'Persilux' and 'Zebra Blinds,' and the barcode label includes 'X003FFPT63.'
recalls.Title: Recall of Persilux Zebra Blinds Due to Strangulation Risks and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Persilux via email at recall@persiluxhome.com or visit their website at https://recall.persiluxhome.com. Additional details can be found by clicking on 'Recall Policy' at the bottom of https://persiluxhome.com.

Images.Caption: "Persilux and Zebra Blinds" is printed on the packaging of the recalled blinds. "X003FFPT63" is printed on the barcode label.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from June 2023 to June 2025, priced between $40 and $124.

Importers.Name: Zhejiang Xidamen New Material Co. Ltd., of China, doing business as Amazon seller Persilu

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords, posing serious injury or death risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately and contact Persilux for a complimentary repair kit, which includes a replacement operating cord wand and installation instructions. To proceed, remove the blinds, cut the operating cord, and email a photo of the blinds without the cord to recall@persiluxhome.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Persilux recalls zebra blinds due to strangulation hazards. Contact for free repair kit and safety information.

recalls.Description: Autoez has issued a recall for its bamboo shades, which are available in brown and measure between 24 to 72 inches in width and 64 to 72 inches in height. Each shade features the brand name "Autoez" on a label located on the top rail. These shades pose serious safety risks due to long operating cords and accessible inner cords that can lead to strangulation and entanglement, particularly affecting children. The shades do not comply with federal safety regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: Recall of Autoez Bamboo Shades Due to Strangulation Risks; Sold on Walmart.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled shades immediately and reach out to Autoez for a complete refund. Contact Autoez via email at Autoez_recall@163.com or visit their website at https://sites.google.com/view/recall-autoez.

Retailers.Name: Available on Walmart.com from September 2023 to May 2025, priced between $21 and $50.

Importers.Name: Sichuan Yunduan Renjia Trading Co. Ltd., of China, doing business as Walmart seller Autoez

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long and accessible cords, posing a risk of serious injury or death to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the shades, contact Autoez for a refund, cut the operating cord, label the shades as 'Recalled', and dispose of them properly. Send a photo of the destroyed shades to Autoez_recall@163.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Autoez recalls bamboo shades due to serious strangulation hazards. Immediate action required for a refund.

recalls.Description: LuckupShein has issued a recall for their window roll up shades due to serious strangulation and entanglement hazards, which pose a risk of injury or death to children. These shades, available in white, gray, and black, come in sizes from 20 to 44 inches wide and 72 inches high, with 'Desheng' labeled on the valance cover. The product does not comply with federal regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: LuckupShein Window Roll Up Shades Recalled for Strangulation Risks and Violations of Federal Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to LuckupShein via email at luckupshein@163.com or visit their website at http://luckupshein.com/recall.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at us.SHEIN.com from May 2023 to December 2024, priced between $34 and $52.

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords

Remedies.Name: Stop using the shades, cut the operating cord, and contact LuckupShein for a free replacement cord device.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: LuckupShein window roll up shades pose serious strangulation risks. Contact for a free replacement remedy.

recalls.Description: BTAMREE is recalling its corded roll up window blinds due to serious safety hazards. These bamboo blinds, available in brown and natural (tan) colors, come in widths from 24 to 72 inches and a height of 72 inches. The brand name 'BTAMREE' can be found on the label sticker. The long operating cords pose a risk of strangulation and entanglement, particularly to children, and violate federal safety regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: BTAMREE Roll Up Window Blinds Recalled for Strangulation and Entanglement Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BTAMREE via email at sales@cy-bamboo.com.

Products.Name: BTAMREE Corded Roll Up Window Blinds

Images.Caption: Recalled BTAMREE Roll Up Window Blinds
Recalled BTAMREE Roll Up Window Blinds
Recalled BTAMREE Roll Up Window Blinds
Recalled BTAMREE Roll Up Window Blinds

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from May 2021 to December 2024, priced between $30 and $100.

Importers.Name: AnJi ChenYiZhuMuZhiPin YouXianGongSi, cy.curtain bright, d/b/a BTMAREE, of China

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords

Remedies.Name: Stop using the blinds immediately, contact BTAMREE for a full refund, mark the product as 'Recalled', and dispose of it. Take a photo of the disposed blinds and send it to sales@cy-bamboo.com to register for a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: BTAMREE recalls roll up window blinds due to strangulation risks. Stop using them and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: Joydeco Roller Window Shades, available on Amazon.com, are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These shades come in various colors such as beige, light gray, and white, with sizes ranging from 20 to 70 inches wide and 75 inches tall. The brand name Joydeco can be found on the label sticker on the headrail. The shades pose a strangulation and entanglement hazard, particularly for children, violating federal safety regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: Joydeco Roller Window Shades Recalled for Strangulation Risks and Non-Compliance with Safety Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Shanghai Chushuo Electronic Commerce Co. Ltd via email at amazonservice@joydeco.com or visit https://recall-joydeco.com for further details. Additional information can be found on their website at www.joydeco.com by clicking on 'Recall Notice' under 'Help & Support.'

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com, sold from September 2021 to September 2024, priced between $26 and $80.

Importers.Name: Shanghai Chushuo Electronic Commerce Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the cords out of children's reach and contact the company for a full refund or a free replacement operating cord wand. A photo of the recalled product with the cord cut must be sent to amazonservice@joydeco.com to receive a repair kit with installation instructions via Shunfeng Express or UPS.

recalls.metaDescription: Joydeco Roller Window Shades recalled for safety hazards. Contact for refunds or replacements. Protect your children from strangulation risks.

recalls.Description: LINKCOO has issued a recall for its corded roller window shades due to significant safety concerns. These shades, available in white, gray, and black, come in widths from 10 to 75 inches and heights of 79 inches. The brand name 'LINKCOO' is visible on the label located on the lower pole of the shades. The shades pose a serious risk of strangulation and entanglement, particularly for children, and do not comply with federal safety regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: LINKCOO Blackout Roller Window Shades Recalled for Strangulation and Entanglement Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers should reach out to LINKCOO via email at recalllinkcoo@163.com or visit http://recall-linkcoo.com/.

Images.Caption: Recalled LINKCOO blackout roller window shades

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from December 2022 until September 2024, priced between $20 and $88.

Distributors.Name: Wenzhou Lingtuo Shangmao Youxiangongsi, dba LINKCOO, of China

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the shades, cut the cord, and contact LINKCOO for a free replacement cord device. A photo of the cut cord product should be sent to recalllinkcoo@163.com to receive the new cord and installation instructions. Amazon will directly notify all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: LINKCOO recalls blackout roller shades due to strangulation hazards. Immediate action required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: Allesin corded roller window shades are being recalled due to serious safety hazards. These shades, available in white, gray, and dark gray, come in widths from 20 to 58 inches and a height of 72 inches. The brand name 'Allesin' is found on the label sticker on the headrail of the shades. The long operating cords pose a strangulation and entanglement risk, especially to children, which is a violation of federal safety regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: Allesin Blackout Roller Window Shades Recalled Due to Strangulation Risks and Violation of Safety Regulations

Manufacturers.Name: Allesin US Store Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from September 2023 to September 2024, priced between $29 and $58

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement risks due to long operating cords

Remedies.Name: Stop using the shades, cut the cord, and request a refund from Allesin

recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using these shades immediately, cut the cord, and contact Allesin for a full refund. To initiate the refund process, consumers need to email a photo of the cut cord product to chinahert_fighting@outlook.com. Refunds will be processed through the original payment method. Amazon is also reaching out to all known purchasers directly.
recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Allesin Blackout Roller Window Shades pose serious strangulation hazards. Immediate action required for refunds.

recalls.Description: Shadeks is recalling ShadesU Roller Window Shades, which were exclusively sold on Amazon.com. These roller blinds come in various colors and sizes, ranging from 20 to 90 inches in width and 72 inches in height. The brand name "ShadesU" can be found on a label sticker located on the right side of the bottom rail of the shades. The shades pose serious safety hazards due to their long operating cords, which can lead to strangulation and entanglement risks for children.
recalls.Title: Shadeks Recalls ShadesU Roller Window Shades Due to Strangulation Risks and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Shadeks at 866-267-4233 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email info@shadesu.com, or visit www.shadesu.com/recall.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from June 2024 to September 2024, priced between $34 and $170.

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the window blind cords away from children and contact Shadeks for return instructions. The company will convert the shades to a cordless design and provide a free shipping label.

recalls.metaDescription: Shadeks recalls ShadesU Roller Window Shades due to strangulation risks. Contact for free repairs and safety updates.

recalls.Description: Homebox Blackout Roller Window Shades, featuring metal cords on the right side, are being recalled due to serious strangulation and entanglement hazards. These shades were available in various colors, including black, white, and sage, and came in sizes ranging from 10 to 70 inches in width and 64 to 72 inches in height. The product label displays the name "Homebox" on the headrail.
recalls.Title: Homebox Blackout Roller Window Shades Recalled for Strangulation Risks; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Zhejiang Mingjing Textile via email at recall@homebox.net.cn or visit their website at http://www.recall.hoteamtex.net.

Images.Caption: Recalled Homebox Blackout Roller Window Shades

Manufacturers.Name: Zhejiang Mingjing Textile Co. Ltd, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from December 2022 to September 2024, priced between $20 and $110.

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords, posing a risk of serious injury or death to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the window blind cords away from children and contact Zhejiang Mingjing Textile for a free repair or replacement. To receive a replacement, upload a photo of the recalled product with the cord cut at http://www.hoteamtex.net/por.jsp?id=2&_jcp=5_2.

recalls.metaDescription: Homebox Blackout Roller Window Shades recalled due to strangulation hazards. Contact Zhejiang Mingjing Textile for free repairs or replacements.

recalls.Description: Thy Trading Company is recalling its bamboo roll up window blinds due to significant safety hazards. These semi-sheer blinds, available in various colors including light brown, brown, and blue, measure approximately 24" wide by 84" high and 72" wide. The blinds are associated with specific SKU numbers found on their packaging, indicating their various styles and colors. The recall is prompted by the potential for serious injury or death due to the long operating cords that pose strangulation and entanglement risks, violating federal regulations for window coverings.
recalls.Title: Thy Trading Company Recalls Bamboo Roll Up Window Blinds Due to Strangulation Risks; Sold Exclusively at Wayfair.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Thy Trading Company at 484-831-5010 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email thytradingllc@outlook.com. More information is available online at https://thytrading.com/recall.html or by visiting www.thytrading.com and selecting "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Thy Trading Semi-Sheer Roll-up Blinds

Retailers.Name: Wayfair.com, sold from June 2024 through September 2024, priced between $11 and $194.

Importers.Name: Thy Trading Company, of Phoenixville, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords

Remedies.Name: Stop using the blinds, contact Thy Trading for a refund, mark the product as 'Recalled', and dispose of it. Take a photo of the destroyed blinds for refund registration.

recalls.metaDescription: Thy Trading recalls bamboo roll up window blinds due to strangulation hazards. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: ChrisDowa Roller Window Shades, available only on Amazon.com, have been recalled due to potential strangulation and entanglement dangers associated with their long operating cords. These shades come in various colors and sizes, measuring between 20-27 inches in width and 72 inches in height. The brand name ChrisDowa is visible on the label sticker located on the headrail.
recalls.Title: ChrisDowa Roller Window Shades Recalled for Strangulation Risks; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, reach out to Shaoxingkeqiao Nenghaozhenfang via email at recallchrisdowa@163.com or visit https://recall-chrisdowa.com/.

Manufacturers.Name: Shaoxingkeqiao Nenghaozhenfang Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from December 2022 through September 2024, priced between $11 and $344.

Hazards.Name: Strangulation and entanglement hazards due to long operating cords, violating federal window covering regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the window blind cords away from children and contact Shaoxingkeqiao Nenghaozhenfang for a free operating cord wand. A photo of the recalled product with the cord cut must be sent to recallchrisdowa@163.com. After verification, a repair kit with installation instructions will be sent via Amazon or FedEx. The company is reaching out to all known buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: ChrisDowa Roller Window Shades recalled due to strangulation hazards. Contact for free repair kit and safety information.

recalls.Description: Foiresoft Basic Zebra Roller Blinds, available in various colors and sizes from 10 to 110 inches wide and 64 inches high, are being recalled due to safety concerns. The product features looped operating cords that can pose a strangulation hazard to young children. The Foiresoft brand is indicated on the label sticker located on the headrail of the blinds.
recalls.Title: Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds Recalled for Strangulation and Entanglement Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Softfunch Inc. at 800-518-4391 from 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. ET, Monday through Thursday, or email recall.foiresoft@gmail.com. More information is available at https://www.foiresoft.co.kr/recall.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from December 2022 to July 2023, priced between $50 and $175.

Hazards.Name: Strangulation hazard due to looped operating cords

Remedies.Name: Stop using the blinds immediately and contact Softfunch Inc. for a full refund. Proof of destruction is required, including a photo of the cut product with cut cords. Dispose of the blinds in your trash. Softfunch will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Foiresoft Zebra Roller Blinds recalled due to strangulation risks. Stop using and contact Softfunch for a refund.
